awards.venue: University of Central Arkansas — Brewer-Hegeman Conference Center, Conway, AR

program.name: Presentation of Individual and Team Awards
Outstanding Service, Star of Tomorrow, and Hall of Fame
program.by: DeeDee Jeffery, Hall of Fame Committee

starOfTomorrow.name: Brooke Bonkoski
starOfTomorrow.bio: A four-year member of the Tri-County USBC youth program at Conway Bowl, Brooke has competed in the State Championship, State Pepsi, Jr. Gold Qualifiers and the Arkansas Challenge with finishes from 1st to 29th. She won the 2025 Arkansas Blue Jr. Gold qualifier (U18G), placed 4th at the 2025 State Youth Championship and 3rd in the State Pepsi. Named to the 2026 ASUSBC All-Star Team, she carries a 185 league average with a 255 high game and 641 series. A Heber Springs High School graduate (4.0 GPA), she plays varsity bowling and volleyball and volunteers as a Bantam/Prep coach.

starOfTomorrow.name: Randall “Carter” Lack
starOfTomorrow.bio: A seven-year member of the Saturday morning youth league at Conway Family Bowl, Carter finished 2nd in singles at the 2025 State Championship and has a first-place finish in the State Pepsi YBC Tournament. He placed 4th in the Arkansas Blue Jr. Gold qualifier and 5th at the Southwest Gold tournament. A member of the Greenbrier High School varsity team (4th in conference and state), he carries a 205 league average (2025-26) with a 300 game and 758 series. A junior with a 3.4 GPA, he also plays in the high school band.

hofInductees2026.name: Michael Burroughs
hofInductees2026.bio: A 50-year bowler and member of the Greater Hot Springs USBC, Michael has competed in State Championship Tournaments for roughly 30 years. He won the AR Championship team title in 2021, the AR Seniors singles title in 2019, and placed 5th at the 2020 USBC National Senior event. He has 27 sanctioned 300 games (including two in one set), an 864 high series, and held a 227 average for multiple seasons. He was 2018 Bowler of the Year.

hofInductees2026.name: Brad Brooks
hofInductees2026.bio: A member of the Central Arkansas USBC and sanctioned bowler for 27 years, Brad won the All-Events Scratch title at the 2018 State Open Championship. He has bowled 18 sanctioned 300 games and 7 sanctioned 800 series (high of 833), with a top league average of 235 held above 220 for 10+ years. A two-time Bowler of the Year and six-time local all-star, he has been a Columbia 300 staffer since 2019 and is an IBPSIA Certified Coach.
